events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. We are assessing trade work: ADAS & post-repair calibration. Observed field condition: alignment done without ADAS recal. Work actively in progress. Reference: OEM position statements. Need choose practical compliant course. Since alignment done without ADAS recalibration is non-compliant and potentially safety critical (ADAS sensors/cameras require recalibration after alignment). Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. It is non-compliant. Risk high or critical? ADAS misalignment can cause collision, so critical maybe. But alignment done without recal is a defect; component condition installed-defective? The ADAS system is installed but not calibrated, so installed-defective and non-compliant. Work stage in-progress. Percent complete maybe 70? Need estimate. Value not asked, 0. Sound etc default. Findings: alignment completed, ADAS recalibration skipped; vehicle/system not compliant with OEM position statements; potential ADAS sensor misalignment. Actions: stop work, perform required ADAS static/dynamic recalibration per OEM procedures; verify calibration with target/scan tool; hold vehicle at quality gate; document. Rationale. References: OEM position statements, maybe SAE J3016? But reference material only OEM position statements. Could include "OEM ADAS calibration position statements". Need JSON valid. Ensure no prose outside JSON.

events.label: response

{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Wheel alignment was completed without the required ADAS recalibration.",
    "ADAS sensors/cameras are installed but not verified to OEM alignment/calibration specs.",
    "Vehicle is non-compliant with OEM position statements and quality gate requirements.",
    "Potential for ADAS misalignment creates a safety-critical driving hazard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the line/vehicle and hold at the current quality gate.",
    "Perform the required ADAS static and/or dynamic recalibration per OEM position statements and service procedures.",
    "Verify calibration using OEM-approved targets, scan tools, and alignment equipment.",
    "Document calibration results and release only after passing the ADAS verification step.",
    "Brief the technician on the requirement that alignment and ADAS recalibration are not separate optional steps."
  ],
  "rationale": "OEM position statements require ADAS recalibration after any wheel alignment or structural repair that can affect sensor orientation. Skipping recalibration leaves the system installed but functionally defective and non-compliant, creating a critical safety risk. The correct journeyman practice is to complete the calibration before releasing the vehicle, not treat it as an optional add-on.",
  "references": [
    "OEM ADAS calibration position statements",
    "OEM service procedures for post-alignment ADAS recalibration"
  ]
}
